description: Required JSON output schema for the spring_summit_2026 CRM contact import audit.

retained_contact_count.notes: Count of canonical person records retained for load.

dropped_unreachable_count.notes: Count of canonical person records dropped because no usable email or phone remains.

duplicate_group_count.notes: Count of person_key groups with more than one source row in the audited batch.

suppressed_contact_ids.notes: Source row IDs excluded because the row is do-not-contact or has revoked consent.

email.normalization: Trim outer whitespace and lowercase. Use an empty string when no canonical email is available.

phone_digits.normalization: Remove non-digit characters from the canonical phone value. Preserve any country code digits present in the source value. Use an empty string when no canonical phone is available.

source_lineage_audit.description: Duplicate-person source lineage evidence for the audited batch.

city_retained_counts.ordering: Include up to the top three retained-contact cities by count descending, then city name ascending for ties.
city_retained_counts.notes: Counts include retained canonical contacts only.

precedence_override_person_keys.description: Duplicate person keys where source precedence changes the selected canonical row compared with choosing the newest row.

suppressed_reachable_row_ids.description: Suppressed rows that still have a usable normalized email or phone.

normalization_changed_row_ids.description: Rows whose nonblank email or phone changes under normalization.

quality_flags.notes: Issue counts for source rows in the audited batch. duplicate_source_rows means source rows beyond the first row inside duplicate person_key groups. suppression_rows counts rows with do_not_contact status or revoked consent. Normalization rows count source rows whose nonblank email or phone changes under the declared normalization.

additional_properties: Allowed, but ignored by the evaluator unless they replace or alter required fields.
